source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane, Mississippi Canyon 582 A

actions_taken: Incident report submitted to BSEE on 28 April 2025
Quotes obtained from vendors to remove crane boom from facility
JSAs updated to provide more thorough description of dropped objects
Barricading of work areas prior to crane work implemented
event.summary: On 16 April 2025 at approximately 1500 hours, a 15-pound cable roller measuring 66 inches long and one inch in diameter broke off from the boom of an East Crane (700L-140) on the Medusa SPAR platform and fell 50 feet to the deck below. The cable roller struck a hydraulic hose on the deck, damaging it beyond use. No injuries occurred. The root causes were inadequate preventive maintenance (failure to repair corroded cable rollers identified in October 2022 inspection) and human error (failure to barricade the area beneath the crane to prevent personnel access to the drop zone).
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations should consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident

Install barricades or other means of preventing personnel from entering potentially hazardous areas in the immediate surroundings and drop zone areas before starting work on cranes where dropped object potential is identified
Provide more thorough description of possible dropped objects in JSAs
Require barricading of work areas prior to performing work on cranes
Remove crane boom from facility to eliminate possibility of additional components falling

threats.name: Cable roller structural failure due to corrosion
Failure to secure drop zone area
Personnel entry into hazardous area beneath crane

consequences.name: Cable roller impact on hydraulic hose

supporting_text: Upon review of the East crane annual inspection performed on 05 October 2022, it was noted that the boom had major corrosion on two of the boom mid-sections and major corrosion on the boom tip section along with storm damage. The inspection also identified that the cable rollers on the boom had major corrosion and were unable to move as designed. The crane inspector recommended that the cable rollers be replaced or repaired within one to three months on the annual crane inspection deficiency report. However, the parts were never ordered by Murphy to repair or replace the cable rollers.

controls.name: Preventive maintenance - cable roller repair/replacement
controls.barrier_role: Repair or replace corroded cable rollers to prevent structural failure
controls.lod_basis: Maintenance deficiency report from annual inspection

supporting_text: The crane inspector recommended that the cable rollers be replaced or repaired within one to three months on the annual crane inspection deficiency report. However, the parts were never ordered by Murphy to repair or replace the cable rollers.

Due to the extensive corrosion that was found on the boom sections and cable rollers during the annual crane inspection on 05 October 2022, more attention should have been directed towards addressing these areas of concern. By failing to address the corrosion issues discovered during the annual crane inspection, an on-going safety concern remained with the possibility of components becoming detached from the crane and falling on personnel or equipment below.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) - dropped object hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify dropped object hazards in work planning
controls.lod_basis: Work planning and hazard identification procedure

supporting_text: Both Murphy's cold work permit and Sparrows' JSA mentioned the potential danger of dropped objects while performing work on the crane.
Failure to properly identify the need to secure the area where the potential for dropped object were present on the Sparrows' JSA, Murphy's Cold Work Permit, and Murphy's Working at Heights Permit.

controls.name: Barricading of drop zone area
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel access to area beneath crane during work

supporting_text: It was noted by the operator that there were no barricades to block off the area underneath the crane to prevent personnel from traveling under the crane while the work was being performed.
By not having the barricades installed around the working area under the crane, it allowed personnel to be within 20 feet of the cable roller when it fell to the deck.

By not having the barricades installed around the working area under the crane, it allowed personnel to be within 20 feet of the cable roller when it fell to the deck. Due to the size and weight of the cable roller that fell 50 feet to the deck below, the possibility of a serious injury or fatality occurring was present.

controls.name: Working at Heights Permit
controls.barrier_role: Control work at height and identify fall/dropped object hazards

supporting_text: As per Murphy's Working at Heights Permit, the only barricades that were installed on the platform were those that were protecting open hole locations.
Failure to properly identify the need to secure the area where the potential for dropped object were present on the Sparrows' JSA, Murphy's Cold Work Permit, and Murphy's Working at Heights Permit.
